set.body:
## Summary

`resident_handle(x)` reuses the resident (GPU) buffer of an already-resident
`adgeMatrix` without copying (documented perf optimization). As a result, the
in-place handle ops (`am_sweep_inplace`, `am_ewise_inplace`) mutate the SOURCE
`adgeMatrix` in place <e2><80><94> and any R-level copy-on-modify alias of it. This breaks
the intuitive R value/COW contract for a caller who never wrote to the source.

This is NOT a correctness regression: the alias-propagation is deliberate and
tested (amatrix-g5r, amatrix-oe8, tests/testthat/test-invariant-residency-machine.R
asserts "sweep rebinds aliases to the new key" and "as.matrix(x) must reflect the
in-place GPU mutation"). The mutable handle is an intentional reference-semantics
escape hatch. The gap is DOCUMENTATION: the `resident_handle` help says it "owns
its resident key" and "reuses the existing device buffer without re-uploading" but
never warns that in-place ops therefore mutate the source object and its aliases.

## Verbatim repro (real MLX backend)

    AMATRIX_MLX_PROBE_GPU=1 Rscript -e '
      suppressMessages({library(Matrix); library(amatrix)})
      amatrix_set_default_precision("fast")
      m <- matrix(as.double(1:12), 3, 4)
      X <- amatrix_bind_resident(adgeMatrix(m, preferred_backend="mlx"), backend="mlx")
      before <- as.matrix(X)[1,1]           # 1
      h <- resident_handle(X, backend="mlx")# reuses X buffer, no re-upload
      am_sweep_inplace(h, 2L, rep(100,4), "+")
      cat("X[1,1] before:", before, " after:", as.matrix(X)[1,1], "\n")
    '
    # Observed: X[1,1] before: 1  after: 101   <- source X silently mutated

Also reproduces via `am_ewise_inplace(h, 2, "*")` (X -> 2*X) and via a plain
COW alias `Y <- X; h <- resident_handle(Y); am_sweep_inplace(h,...)` (mutates X too,
since X and Y share one object_id / registry entry).

Does NOT reproduce when the source is not yet resident: `resident_handle()` then
uploads its own fresh copy and the source stays independent (verified).

## Expected / desired

Not a behavior change to the handle contract. Options, in order of preference:
1. Add a prominent COW/aliasing warning to the `resident_handle` roxygen ("mutating
   the handle mutates the source matrix and its aliases; pass a copy or a not-yet-
   resident matrix to isolate").
2. Optionally offer `resident_handle(x, copy = TRUE)` (or a `clone=`) that forces a
   fresh device buffer instead of reusing the source key, for callers who want value
   semantics.

## Provenance

Found during copy-on-modify investigation amatrix-2f2 (round-6 bug hunt). The
implicit COW path (`Y <- X; Y[1,1] <- 999`, diag<-, logical mask, column assign,
dense + sparse, eager + deferred MLX) is SAFE; only the explicit mutable handle API
aliases the source.
set.title: Document resident_handle COW aliasing: in-place handle ops mutate the source adgeMatrix
